title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
code: library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  slow_fcn <- function(x) {   Sys.sleep(0.1)  # emulate work   x^2 }  xs <- 1:100 ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify()

title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: vignette demonstrates use approach add progress reporting functions lapply(), tapply(), apply(), replicate() base package. example, consider base R lapply() function, commonly used apply function elements vector list, : lapply() provides feedback far progressed, can easily add progress reporting, using: Using default progress handler, progress reporting appear :
code: xs <- 1:100 ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |=====                    |  20%

title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: progressify() function supports following base package functions: lapply(), vapply(), sapply(), tapply() mapply(), .mapply(), Map() eapply() apply() replicate() ()

title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: progressify package works together futurize package. can parallelize add progress reporting single pipeline:
code: library(futurize) plan(multisession) library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> futurize() |> progressify()

title: Progress updates for base-R apply functions
text: BiocGenerics package defines generic functions lapply(), sapply(), mapply(), tapply(). S4 generic functions override non-generic, counterpart functions base package. BiocGenerics attached, solution specify base version wish progressify, .e.
code: y <- base::lapply(1:3, sqrt) |> progressify()

code: library(future.apply) plan(multisession)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- future_lapply(xs, slow_fcn) library(future.apply) plan(multisession)  library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  ys <- future_lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |=====                    |  20%

code: library(purrr) xs <- 1:100 ys <- map(xs, slow_fcn) xs <- 1:100 ys <- xs |> map(slow_fcn) library(purrr)  library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- xs |> map(slow_fcn) |> progressify() |=====                    |  20%

title: Progress updates for 'plyr' functions
text: vignette demonstrates use approach add progress reporting plyr functions llply(), maply(), ddply(). plyr llply() function commonly used apply function elements list return list. example, llply() provides feedback far progressed, can easily add progress reporting, using: Using default progress handler, progress reporting appear :
code: library(plyr) xs <- 1:100 ys <- llply(xs, slow_fcn) library(plyr)  library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- llply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |=====                    |  20%

title: Progress updates for 'plyr' functions
text: progressify() function supports following plyr functions: l_ply(), laply(), ldply(), llply() m_ply(), maply(), mdply(), mlply() r_ply(), raply(), rdply(), rlply()

title: Progress updates for 'foreach' functions
code: library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE) library(foreach)  slow_fcn <- function(x) {   Sys.sleep(0.1)  # emulate work   x^2 }  xs <- 1:100 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% slow_fcn(x) |> progressify()

title: Progress updates for 'foreach' functions
text: vignette demonstrates use approach add progress reporting foreach foreach() construct doFuture %dofuture% operator. example, consider: foreach() construct provides feedback far progressed. can easily add progress reporting piping progressify(): Using default progress handler, progress reporting appear :
code: library(foreach) xs <- 1:100 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% slow_fcn(x) library(foreach)  library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% slow_fcn(x) |> progressify() |=====                    |  20%

title: Progress updates for 'foreach' functions
text: approach works doFuture package parallel foreach evaluation:
code: library(doFuture) plan(multisession)  library(progressify) library(progressr) handlers(global = TRUE)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %dofuture% slow_fcn(x) |> progressify()

title: Progress updates for 'foreach' functions
text: progressify() function supports following foreach operators: foreach(...) %% { ... } foreach(...) %dopar% { ... } following doFuture operator: foreach(...) %dofuture% { ... }

title: Authors and Citation
text: Henrik Bengtsson. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bengtsson H (2026). progressify: Progress Updates Everywhere. R package version 0.0.0-9013.
code: @Manual{,   title = {progressify: Progress Updates Everywhere},   author = {Henrik Bengtsson},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0-9013}, }

title: Progress Updates Everywhere
text: progressify package makes extremely simple report progress updates existing map-reduce calls. need know single function called progressify() take care everything, e.g. progressify() function signals progress updates via progressr package, meaning can choose among supported progressr handlers render progress updates, whether via terminal, progress bar, even sound. progressify package one hard dependency - progressr package. addition getting progress updates via progressr, using progressify() also get access benefits comes progressr. Notably, full control progress reported, works seamlessly across different environments frontends.
code: y <- lapply(x, fcn) |> progressify() y <- map(x, fcn) |> progressify() y <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { fcn(x) } |> progressify()

title: Progress Updates Everywhere
text: progressify package supports transpilation functions multiple packages. table summarizes supported map-reduce functions. Table 1: Map-reduce functions currently supported progressify() progress reporting. examples:
code: library(progressify) handlers("txtprogressbar")  xs <- 1:10 ys <- lapply(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()  xs <- 1:10 ys <- purrr::map(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()  library(foreach) xs <- 1:10 ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) } |> progressify()  xs <- 1:10 ys <- plyr::llply(xs, function(x) { Sys.sleep(0.1); sqrt(x) }) |> progressify()

title: Progress Updates Everywhere
text: progressify package compatible futurize package, parallelizes code via futureverse. can combine two get progress reporting parallelization:
code: library(progressify) library(futurize) plan(multisession)  xs <- 1:100 ys <- lapply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()  ys <- purrr::map(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()  library(foreach) ys <- foreach(x = xs) %do% { slow_fcn(x) } |> progressify() |> futurize()  ys <- plyr::llply(xs, slow_fcn) |> progressify() |> futurize()

what: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
text: Evaluate regular map-reduce call parallel

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
code: progressify(   expr,   substitute = TRUE,   ...,   when = TRUE,   eval = TRUE,   envir = parent.frame() )

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
text: expr R expression. substitute TRUE, expr quoted. TRUE (default), expression progressified, otherwise . eval TRUE (default), progressified expression evaluated, returned. envir environment expr evaluated. ... used.

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
text: Returns value evaluated expression expr.

title: Evaluate a regular map-reduce call in parallel — progressify
code: xs <- list(1, 1:2, 1:2, 1:5) y <- lapply(X = xs, FUN = sum) |> progressify() str(y) #> List of 4 #>  $ : num 1 #>  $ : int 3 #>  $ : int 3 #>  $ : int 15

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: Transpile R expression

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
code: transpile(   expr,   options = list(...),   ...,   when = TRUE,   eval = TRUE,   envir = parent.frame(),   disable = FALSE,   type = "built-in",   what = "transpile",   unwrap = list(base::`{`, base::`(`, base::`!`, base::local, base::I, base::identity,     base::invisible, base::suppressMessages, base::suppressWarnings,     base::suppressPackageStartupMessages),   debug = FALSE )

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: expr R expression, typically function call transpile. FALSE, transpiler disabled, TRUE, re-enabled. NA, TRUE returned transpiler enabled, otherwise FALSE. options (optional) Named options transpilation. TRUE (default), expression transpiled, otherwise . eval TRUE (default), transpiled expression evaluated, otherwise returned. envir environment expression evaluated. type Type transpiler use. unwrap (optional) list functions considered wrapping functions transpiler unwrap ("enter"). allows us transpile expressions within { ... } local( ... ).

title: Transpile an R expression — transpile
text: Returns value evaluated expression expr eval = TRUE, otherwise transpiled expression. expr NA, TRUE returned transpiler enabled, otherwise FALSE.

title: Version (development version)
text: retake previous, proof--concept version. Starting version, now borrowing futurize package, implements closely related “parallelization” transpilers.

title: Version (development version)
text: Add support map-reduce CRAN package doFuture, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %dofuture% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package foreach, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package furrr, e.g. y <- furrr::future_map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support map-reduce CRAN package future.apply, e.g. y <- future.apply::future_lapply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support plyr, e.g. y <- plyr::llply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support purrr, e.g. y <- purrr::map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support replicate(), e.g. y <- replicate(n, rnorm(10)) |> progressify().

title: Version 0.0.1
text: progressify() function makes common map-reduce functions report progress.ons relayed, among things. Add support base R, e.g. y <- lapply(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support purrr, e.g. y <- map(xs, fcn) |> progressify(). Add support foreach, e.g. y <- foreach(x = xs) %% { fcn(x) } |> progressify(). Add support plyr, e.g. y <- llply(xs, fcn) |> progressify().
